meeting_context: Regular Rental Housing Committee meeting held on January 9, 2023 by video conference. The committee completed roll call, approved a consent calendar item authorizing continued virtual meetings under AB 361, received no public comments, heard Chair Haines-Livesay announce that the end-of-January meeting would be her last because she would no longer meet the residency requirement in February, and discussed a possible scheduling conflict for the next meeting.

points.text: Andrea Kennedy conducted roll call. Grunwald and Pardo Dazella were absent, while Rosas, Almond, Ramos, and Chair Haines-Livesay were present.
points.source: Andrea Kennedy: We will begin with a committee member Grunwald, who is absent. Andrea Kennedy: Next we will call on committee member Pardo Dazella, who is also absent. Andrea Kennedy: Now we have committee member Rosas. Andrea Kennedy: Committee member Almond. Andrea Kennedy: Vice Chair Ramos. Andrea Kennedy: And Chair Haynes, thank you.
points.speaker: Andrea Kennedy

points.text: Haines-Livesay called the meeting to order at 7:02 PM and noted that all committee members would participate by video conference, with public viewing available through Legistar, YouTube, and Comcast Channel 26.
points.source: Haines-Livesay: All members of the Rental Housing Committee will participate in the meeting by video conference with no physical meeting location. Haines-Livesay: Members of the public wishing to observe the meeting live may do so at mountainview.legistar.com, on YouTube, at mountainview.gov/youtube, and on Comcast Channel 26. Haines-Livesay: The meeting is called to order at 7 O 2:00 PM.
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

points.text: No committee member pulled the consent calendar item for discussion, and no public comments were received on the item.
points.source: Haines-Livesay: Would any member of the committee like to pull an item OK? Haines-Livesay: I'm not seeing any hands now. Haines-Livesay: Would any member of the public on the line like to provide comment on this item? Haines-Livesay: OK, not seeing any public comment?
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

points.text: The committee unanimously approved the consent calendar item adopting a resolution to continue virtual meetings pursuant to Brown Act provisions as amended by AB 361.
points.source: Almond: I'll make a motion to adopt A resolution to continue virtual meetings pursuant to Brown Act provisions as amended by AB 361. Ramos: I'll second it. Andrea Kennedy: Motion passes unanimously.
points.speaker: Andrea Kennedy

points.text: No public comments were received during oral communications on non-agenda items.
points.source: Haines-Livesay: Would any member of the public on the line like to provide comment on non agenda items? Haines-Livesay: I'm not seeing any public comment now.
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

points.text: Chair Haines-Livesay announced that the end-of-January meeting would be her last because she would no longer meet the residency requirement in February.
points.source: Haines-Livesay: I just wanted to let the committee and the public know that regretfully, next meeting, the meeting at the end of January will be my last meeting because I will have to step down and February I won't meet the residency test.
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

points.text: Committee members responded with appreciation and regret at Haines-Livesay's departure, including comments from Rosas, Anky van Deursen, Almond, and Ramos.
points.source: Rosas: We'll miss you. Anky van Deursen: Wonderful to have you as a chair. Almond: I enjoyed serving with you when I was chair and you were vice chair. Ramos: I don't want you to leave.
points.speaker: Committee Members

points.text: In response to a question about the next meeting date, Anky van Deursen said she would poll committee members because City Council had scheduled an extra meeting on January 30, creating a possible conflict with the Rental Housing Committee meeting.
points.source: Almond: Do we have a new meeting date for end of January? Anky van Deursen: I will pull all the Rental Housing Committee members separately to see if the date needs to be changed. Anky van Deursen: It seems like the City Council just planned an extra meeting for January 30, which is the same date as we have a Rental Housing Committee meeting.
points.speaker: Anky van Deursen

points.text: Haines-Livesay said the next Rental Housing Committee meeting was tentatively set for Monday, January 30 at 7:00 PM, but that the date remained to be determined.
points.source: Haines-Livesay: This Rental housing committee meeting is scheduled to be held maybe on Monday, January 30th at 7:00 PM or maybe a different day. Haines-Livesay: TBA on that.
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

points.source: Haines-Livesay: The meeting is adjourned at 7 O 8:00 PM.
points.speaker: Haines-Livesay

decisions.text: The committee unanimously adopted a resolution to continue virtual meetings pursuant to Brown Act provisions as amended by AB 361.
decisions.source: Almond: I'll make a motion to adopt A resolution to continue virtual meetings pursuant to Brown Act provisions as amended by AB 361. Ramos: I'll second it. Andrea Kennedy: Motion passes unanimously.
